sourceMovie.title: The Banker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Banker (2020) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. George Nolfi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Green Book
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Banker clicked for you, Green Book (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Big Short
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Banker clicked for you, The Big Short (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dog Day Afternoon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Banker clicked for you, Dog Day Afternoon (1975) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: CODA
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Banker clicked for you, CODA (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Banker?
faq.answer: Try Green Book, The Big Short, Dog Day Afternoon—each shares DNA with The Banker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Banker?
faq.answer: The Banker (2020) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Banker worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
